page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU cores with multithreading enabled. The system is configured with 16 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk operations.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25% at 2693.38. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall within the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9379.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 22027.2. Memory read bandwidth reaches 1018.3 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 75.4 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while write bandwidth is measured at 40.36 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput averages 15.62 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations execute at 16.05 million operations per second.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-16 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. However, this resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints, as users avoid paying for unallocated RAM. This instance is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, web serving, batch processing, and lightweight machine learning inference, as demonstrated by its 135M parameter LLM prompt processing speed of 904.0 tokens per second.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 physical cores. This configuration provides a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. The instance achieves a static web serving throughput of 15.62 GB/sec and a Redis SET rate of 16.05 million operations per second. Due to its high compute density and limited memory allocation, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks. It is highly suited for software compilation, web serving, batch processing, and lightweight text processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core CPU performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-16 features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a streamlined memory profile for efficient batch processing and web serving.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
16 GB of system memory providing 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and batch processing
